title: plantago subulata subsp. holosteum
intro: Plantago subulata subsp. holosteum, also known as prickly psyllium or wild beet, is a subspecies of the Plantago subulata plant, a type of flowering plant in the plantain family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its high content of soluble fiber, which can help regulate bowel movements and ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. The health benefits of Plantago subulata subsp. holosteum include its ability to support healthy gut bacteria, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural laxative. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or extracting the plant's active compounds using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
